note: FRESH BLIND BENCHMARK — Questions only. Zero answers included anywhere.

questions.prompt: There are three boxes: one contains only apples, one contains only oranges, and one contains both apples and oranges. All three boxes are mislabeled. You pick one fruit from one box without looking inside. You pick an apple. Which box did you pick from? Explain your reasoning.
A fair coin is flipped 10 times. What is the probability that it lands heads exactly 5 times? Give your answer as a simplified fraction.
All A are B. Some B are C. No C are D. Therefore, which of the following must be true? (a) Some A are D (b) No A is D (c) Some A are C (d) Nothing can be concluded about A and D. Explain.
A train leaves Station A at 60 mph. Another train leaves Station B (120 miles away) at 40 mph heading toward Station A. If they start at the same time, how far from Station A do they meet?
A cube is painted red on all six faces. It is then cut into 27 identical smaller cubes (3x3x3). How many of the small cubes have exactly two faces painted red?

Write a Python function 'find_duplicates(lst)' that returns a list of all elements that appear more than once in the input list, preserving the order of their first occurrence. Include type hints and a docstring.
Write a Python function 'merge_intervals(intervals)' that takes a list of intervals [start, end] and merges all overlapping intervals. Example: [[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]] -> [[1,6],[8,10],[15,18]].

Write a Python function 'deep_flatten(nested)' that takes a list of arbitrarily nested lists and returns a flat list. Use recursion. Example: deep_flatten([1, [2, [3, 4], 5], 6]) -> [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6].
Write a Python function 'lru_cache(maxsize=128)' that returns a decorator implementing a least-recently-used cache with the given max size. Include type hints.

Write a Python function 'top_k_frequent(words, k)' that returns the k most frequent words from a list of strings, sorted by frequency (descending) then alphabetically. Example: top_k_frequent(['the','day','is','sunny','the','the','sunny','is','is'], 2) -> ['is', 'the'].

Explain the CAP theorem in distributed systems. Can a distributed database guarantee all three properties simultaneously? If not, which combinations are possible and what are real-world examples of each?
What is the difference between TCP and UDP? Give three concrete examples of applications that use each protocol and explain why they chose that protocol.
What is the time complexity of quicksort in the best, average, and worst cases? Under what conditions does each case occur, and how can the worst case be avoided?
Explain the bias-variance tradeoff in machine learning. What happens when a model is underfitting vs overfitting, and what techniques address each?
What happens when you type 'https://www.google.com' into a browser and press Enter? Describe the full chain from keystroke to page render.
